# Osteoperionews

> Weekly curated English & Italian summaries of open-access periodontology, dental-implantology and peri-implant research, by Dr. Ernesto Bruschi (periodontist, implantologist, oral surgeon; ORCID 0000-0002-4773-5384). Each entry is an original short summary of a peer-reviewed paper that links to the primary source (DOI and PubMed). English pages are under /en, Italian under /it.

- Site: https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com
- Author: Dr. Ernesto Bruschi — https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4773-5384
- Feeds: https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/feed.xml (EN), https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/feed-it.xml (IT)
- Full archive: https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles (EN), https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/it/articles (IT)

## Articles

- [At 20 years implants hold up like natural teeth — but only with maintenance](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/correlation-between-periodontal-parameters-at-teeth-and-implants-an-up-to-20-yea-42320591): In brief: Over 20 years, implants and contra-lateral teeth showed comparable attachment loss (~4.5 vs ~4.4 mm) and survival in maintained periodontitis… — Journal of dentistry (DOI: 10.1016/j.jdent.2026.106848)
- [Antibiotics add half a millimetre in peri-implantitis — local beats systemic](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/efficacy-of-adjunctive-antibiotics-compared-to-non-antibiotic-therapies-followin-42348534): In brief: Across 22 RCTs, adjunctive antibiotics improved PPD by ~0.69 mm and CAL by ~0.55 mm over debridement alone — statistically significant,… — PloS one (DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0352311)
- [Xenogeneic blocks match autogenous bone at 4 years — except in the esthetic zone](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/clinical-outcomes-of-implants-in-atrophic-ridges-augmented-with-collagenated-xen-42339863): In brief: At 4 years, collagenated xenogeneic and autogenous bone blocks gave comparable implant survival and success after lateral ridge augmentation. — Brazilian oral research (DOI: 10.1590/1807-3107bor-2026.vol40.030)
- [Fascia lata allograft thickens peri-implant mucosa without a palatal harvest](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/fascia-lata-allograft-versus-subepithelial-connective-tissue-graft-for-peri-impl-42337524): In brief: In a 16-patient pilot RCT, fascia lata allograft and connective tissue graft both significantly increased peri-implant mucosal thickness and… — BMC oral health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08829-y)
- [Patients on Ozempic-class drugs: a risk modifier in perio, not a treatment](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/glp-1-receptor-agonists-in-periodontology-mechanisms-clinical-evidence-and-impli-42352323): In brief: GLP-1 receptor agonists may affect periodontal tissues both indirectly (glycemic control, weight, inflammation) and directly (GLP-1R / DPP-4… — Biomolecules (DOI: 10.3390/biom16060857)
- [Past 10 cigarettes a day, GBR implants lose twice the bone](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/effect-of-smoking-intensity-on-clinical-outcomes-of-implants-placed-with-simulta-42309675): In brief: In this 5-year cohort, smokers of >10 cigarettes/day lost about twice the marginal bone (2.67 mm) of non- and light smokers (1.34-1.40 mm). — Journal of oral science (DOI: 10.2334/josnusd.26-0028)
- [PEEK or titanium mesh for the ridge? They tie once you control the graft](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/assessment-of-two-treatment-protocols-using-prebent-titanium-mesh-and-customized-42298522): In brief: RCT in 14 patients (28 sites): no significant difference in horizontal bone gain between prebent titanium mesh (3.02 mm) and customized PEEK… — BMC oral health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08720-w)
- [Thin-phenotype lower recessions: the tunnel beats the envelope flap](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/a-short-term-randomized-controlled-trial-of-bilaminar-techniques-using-tunnel-ve-42304495): In brief: RCT in 59 patients (103 lower-incisor sites, thin phenotype): at 6 months the tunnel + CTG gave 79% mean root coverage vs 58% for the envelope… — Head & face medicine (DOI: 10.1186/s13005-026-00631-z)
- [Putting a number on periodontitis as cardiovascular risk: a proposed score](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/periodontal-inflammation-a-clinically-significant-and-underrecognized-cardiovasc-42292687): In brief: A narrative review proposing the Endothelial Risk Score (ERS): it combines Periodontal Inflamed Surface Area (PISA) with age, sex, systolic BP,… — American heart journal plus : cardiology research and practice (DOI: 10.1016/j.ahjo.2026.100808)
- [Oral disease leads NCD prevalence — and periodontal disability rises with wealth](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/the-burden-of-oral-disorders-in-latin-america-and-caribbean-countries-from-1990-42290709): In brief: In 2023, oral disorders in Latin America and the Caribbean caused 308 million prevalent cases and 2.41 million years lived with disability —… — Lancet regional health. Americas (DOI: 10.1016/j.lana.2026.101517)
- [GBR membranes: a 26% complication rate forces a rethink beyond passive occlusion](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/design-and-applications-of-barrier-membranes-for-guided-bone-regeneration-42220644): In brief: A meta-analysis of 100 studies puts GBR complication rates near 26%, exposing the structural limits of conventional collagen and PTFE membranes. — Bioactive Materials (DOI: 10.1016/j.bioactmat.2026.05.008)
- [Augmented vs native posterior mandible: which bone hosts healthier implants at 15 years?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/outcomes-of-dental-implants-placed-in-horizontally-augmented-or-native-posterior-42225503): In brief: Implant survival was nearly identical at 15 years: 94.7% in native bone, 95.3% in augmented bone. — International Journal of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery (DOI: 10.1016/j.ijom.2026.05.015)
- [Peri-implantitis debridement: less than 1 mm cleaned in this SEM cohort](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/guidelines-for-the-depth-of-surface-decontamination-during-peri-implantitis-trea-42252880): In brief: SEM analysis of 49 explanted implants found a mean contaminant-free zone of just 0.66 mm (range 0–3.0 mm). — Journal of Periodontal & Implant Science (DOI: 10.5051/jpis.2501940097)
- [Perio therapy as CVD prevention: cost-effective only over a lifetime horizon](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/cost-effectiveness-analysis-of-non-surgical-periodontal-treatment-to-prevent-str-42271428): In brief: Over a 25-year horizon, NSPT reached an ICER of €19,322/QALY — within NICE thresholds; at 10 years the ICER more than doubled to €49,847. — Cost Effectiveness and Resource Allocation (DOI: 10.1186/s12962-026-00786-1)
- [EMD at 30: where the evidence holds — and where it does not](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/three-decades-of-enamel-matrix-derivative-from-dental-innovation-to-extra-oral-a-42254883): In brief: EMD remains a validated adjunct for recession coverage and intrabony defects, with consistent evidence across 30 years of literature. — International Journal of Dentistry (DOI: 10.1155/ijod/6556335)
- [Full-arch implants: what 29 Italian experts agreed on — and what it changes in practice](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/consensus-statement-on-full-arch-implant-rehabilitations-evidence-based-recommen-42194656): In brief: Four implants in the mandibular full-arch show comparable survival to six, per the consensus statements. — Journal of Clinical Medicine (DOI: 10.3390/jcm15103695)
- [Regeneration then orthodontics: 20-year tooth survival in Stage IV periodontitis](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/long-term-outcomes-of-regenerative-periodontal-therapy-and-orthodontic-treatment-42214655): In brief: In this cohort, 89.1% estimated tooth survival at 20 years for severely compromised teeth (PPD ≥7 mm, intrabony defect, pathologic migration)… — Journal of Dentistry (DOI: 10.1016/j.jdent.2026.106799)
- [Short implants at 10 years: viable for the atrophic ridge, but survival stays below standard implants](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/ten-year-outcomes-of-short-dental-implants-6-mm-a-systematic-review-and-sensitiv-42226287): In brief: Pooled 10-year survival was 91–94% (patient/implant level); sensitivity analysis returned the more conservative 89.7–92.8%. — BMC Oral Health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08742-4)
- [Simultaneous GBR with a 3D preformed PLGA membrane: 87% bone gain maintained at 5 months, zero exposures](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/simultaneous-implant-placement-with-guided-bone-regeneration-for-horizontal-ridg-42240865): In brief: In this 21-site single-arm study, mean horizontal bone augmentation was 2.99 mm with 87.2% of hard tissue gain preserved at 5 months. — Clinical Oral Investigations (DOI: 10.1007/s00784-026-06959-9)
- [Connective Tissue Graft Under Pontics: Early Gain, Uncertain Long-Term Edge](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/volumetric-changes-at-pontic-sites-after-connective-tissue-grafting-a-systematic-42194803): In brief: CTG reliably improves ridge contour and soft-tissue volume at pontic sites in the short term. — Journal of Clinical Medicine (DOI: 10.3390/jcm15103842)
- [Full-arch implant prostheses: the implants survive, but material choice drives complication risk](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/technical-and-biological-complications-of-implant-supported-fixed-complete-dentu-42129020): In brief: Prosthesis survival was 92.3% and implant loss 4.2% over a mean 6.8 years — high reliability in this cohort. — International Journal of Implant Dentistry (DOI: 10.1186/s40729-026-00689-z)
- [Periodontal disease starts in childhood — but Asia's data can't yet prove how often](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/prevalence-of-periodontal-diseases-in-children-and-adolescents-in-asian-countrie-42178105): In brief: Gingivitis was the dominant finding across 84 studies from 24 Asian countries, with prevalence spanning under 10% to 100% — a range that… — Journal of Dentistry (DOI: 10.1016/j.jdent.2026.106790)
- [Socket Shield: FEA points to 120-150° arc as the biomechanical sweet spot for the retained fragment](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/biomechanical-effects-of-implant-root-fragment-contact-in-the-socket-shield-tech-42172860): In brief: In this finite element model, a labial arc angle of 120-150° for the retained root fragment produced the most balanced stress distribution… — International Dental Journal (DOI: 10.1016/j.identj.2026.109623)
- [Zirconia vs titanium for overdentures: a 14-point success gap and more bone loss at 3 years](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/a-3-year-split-mouth-randomized-controlled-clinical-trial-of-zirconia-versus-tit-42188380): In brief: In this 3-year split-mouth RCT, titanium implant success (91.7%) outpaced zirconia (77.8%) by nearly 14 percentage points. — Journal of Functional Biomaterials (DOI: 10.3390/jfb17050213)
- [Autonomous robotics for transcrestal sinus elevation: submillimetre trueness in a first cohort](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/autonomous-dental-implant-robotics-for-transcrestal-maxillary-sinus-floor-elevat-42174584): In brief: In this 12-implant cohort, autonomous robotic placement achieved mean global deviations of 0.58 mm (coronal) and 0.64 mm (apical), with 1.19°… — BMC Oral Health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08381-9)
- [SRP alone beats the adjunct: NaOCl/AA gel adds no microbiological benefit in this RCT](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/real-time-qpcr-evaluation-of-amino-acids-buffered-sodium-hypochlorite-gel-as-an-42185451): In brief: A single 30-second NaOCl/AA gel application during SRP produced no additional microbiological benefit over mechanical debridement alone. — Scientific reports (DOI: 10.1038/s41598-026-54584-z)
- [PRF and platelet concentrates in bone regeneration: adjunct, not replacement — what the evidence actually supports](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/platelet-concentrates-in-alveolar-and-periodontal-bone-regeneration-adjunctive-b-42194580): In brief: L-PRF and A-PRF show clinically relevant adjunctive benefit in periodontal intrabony defects, correlating with improved CAL gain and faster soft… — Journal of clinical medicine (DOI: 10.3390/jcm15103617)
- [Free gingival graft donor site: CTA alone shortens closure time and reduces mid-healing pain](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/impact-of--cyanoacrylate-tissue-adhesive-on-soft-tissue-healing-at-donor-sites-i-42174629): In brief: In this 60-patient RCT, α-cyanoacrylate adhesive alone shortened intraoperative wound closure time significantly vs both suture-based groups… — Head & face medicine (DOI: 10.1186/s13005-026-00627-9)
- [Plasma-activated healing abutments: a histological signal at the epithelial interface](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/histological-evaluation-of-the-soft-tissue-response-to-plasma-activated-healing-42178231): In brief: Plasma activation of healing abutments produced lower inflammation scores in the basal zone only (P = 0.04); upper and intermediate zones showed… — Journal of prosthodontic research (DOI: 10.2186/jpr.JPR_D_25_00207)
- [Implants at 20 years: a 93% survival rate — but half of failures are mechanical, not biological](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/dental-implant-survival-and-marginal-bone-loss-after-a-minimum-of-20-years-syste-42173763): In brief: Pooled 20-year survival was 93.0% (CI 91.6–94.1%) across 1,028 implants in prospective studies — a reassuring baseline figure for patient… — International journal of oral and maxillofacial surgery (DOI: 10.1016/j.ijom.2026.05.011)
- [Implants in diabetes: HbA1c below 7% is the threshold, not the diagnosis](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/dental-implant-outcomes-in-patients-with-diabetes-mellitus-a-systematic-review-42177496): In brief: Well-controlled diabetics (HbA1c ≤7%) showed outcomes comparable to non-diabetic patients in this review. — BMC oral health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-07782-0)
- [Mandibular bone repair: the periosteal stem-cell switch that could replace grafts](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/the-plagl1-klf4-irx5-axis-promotes-the-osteogenesis-of-periosteal-progenitors-du-42168182): In brief: The PLAGL1-KLF4-IRX5 transcription factor axis controls osteoblast differentiation of periosteal stem/progenitor cells in the mandible. — Nature communications (DOI: 10.1038/s41467-026-73058-4)
- [Gut dysbiosis as a causal driver of periodontal bone loss: FMT evidence in a mouse model](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/ulcerative-colitis-driven-gut-dysbiosis-exacerbates-periodontal-bone-loss-throug-42173912): In brief: In a combined UC + periodontitis mouse model, gut dysbiosis causally doubled alveolar bone loss via fecal microbiota transplantation. — NPJ biofilms and microbiomes (DOI: 10.1038/s41522-026-01015-6)
- [Dentine vs e-PRF for GBR: equivalent outcomes, your choice](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/dentine-derived-barrier-membrane-versus-extended-platelet-rich-fibrin-for-guided-42168967): In brief: In this 40-patient RCT, DDBM and e-PRF produced identical implant survival (100%) and marginal bone loss (~1.2–1.3 mm buccal) at 24 months. — BMC oral health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08582-2)
- [Tissue-level implants at 25 years: prosthesis design doesn't drive bone loss — suppuration does](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/crestal-bone-loss-and-peri-implant-conditions-at-tissue-level-implants-influence-42132365): In brief: After 25 years, crestal bone loss and peri-implantitis prevalence did not differ significantly between single crowns, splinted crowns, or… — Clinical implant dentistry and related research (DOI: 10.1111/cid.70158)
- [ISQ at placement: useful stability indicator, but not a predictor of long-term survival](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/resonance-frequency-analysis-and-clinical-outcomes-in-implant-dentistry-a-system-42117690): In brief: ISQ shows a moderate, statistically significant correlation with insertion torque (pooled r = 0.44) across 20 pooled studies, but shared… — Clinical implant dentistry and related research (DOI: 10.1111/cid.70156)
- [Attached gingiva width: not an independent bone-loss driver in this 5-year cohort](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/association-between-attached-gingiva-width-and-peri-implant-marginal-bone-loss-a-42129781): In brief: In univariate analysis, implants with <2 mm attached gingiva showed greater mean bone loss (2.17 vs 1.64 mm), but this difference did not… — BMC oral health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08572-4)
- [FGG vs. collagen matrix for peri-implant keratinized tissue: autograft wins at 6 months](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/evaluation-of-soft-tissue-outcomes-following-keratinized-tissue-augmentation-aro-42109340): In brief: In this RCT, FGG produced significantly greater keratinized tissue width and soft tissue thickness than XCM at all three follow-up points (1, 3,… — Bioinformation (DOI: 10.6026/973206300221193)
- [Peri-implantitis destroys faster with less bacteria: the vascular signature that explains it](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/cd38-endothelial-remodeling-vasculopathy-periodontitis-peri-implantitis-42103720): In brief: In this large single-cell dataset (967,000+ cells), peri-implantitis tissues showed lower bacterial load and microbial diversity than… — Nature Communications (DOI: 10.1038/s41467-026-72452-2)
- [Naringin curbs alveolar bone loss in rat periodontitis — via NLRP3 and the gut axis](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/naringin-periodontitis-ampk-nrf2-nlrp3-gut-microbiota-42135319): In brief: In a rat ligature model, naringin reduced alveolar bone loss and suppressed NLRP3 inflammasome activation via the AMPK/Nrf2 pathway. — NPJ Biofilms and Microbiomes (DOI: 10.1038/s41522-026-01007-6)
- [Allogeneic bone plug vs DBBM for GBR: comparable bone, faster turnover in pilot RCT](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/allogeneic-bone-plug-vs-dbbm-guided-bone-regeneration-rct-42126621): In brief: At 6 months, vital bone fraction did not differ significantly between ACBP and DBBM (38.8% vs 32.2%, P=0.105) in this pilot RCT. — Clinical Oral Investigations (DOI: 10.1007/s00784-026-06914-8)
- [One-piece vs. two-piece implants at 15+ years: better bone, but at what cost?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/one-piece-two-piece-implants-15-17-year-rct-follow-up-42126189): In brief: Overall implant survival reached 95% at 15-17 years, with two-piece implants showing higher survival (98.0%) than one-piece (91.8%) in this… — Clinical Implant Dentistry and Related Research (DOI: 10.1111/cid.70157)
- [Silver-coated implants curb bone loss in peri-implantitis — but only in mice, so far](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/silver-ion-coated-implants-suppress-peri-implantitis-murine-42135431): In brief: Ag⁺ ions on a hydroxyapatite/IP6 coating showed significant antibacterial activity against P. gingivalis in vitro. — Scientific Reports (DOI: 10.1038/s41598-026-53121-2)
- [Periodontitis and muscle loss: gingival activin A as a systemic mediator](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/periodontitis-induces-skeletal-muscle-atrophy-by-increasing-circulating-levels-o-42091891): In brief: In a mouse model, periodontitis reduced skeletal muscle mass through elevated circulating activin A, independent of food intake or fat mass… — Nature communications (DOI: 10.1038/s41467-026-72766-1)
- [Healthy implants, higher MMP-8: subclinical tissue stress before any clinical sign](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/clinical-parameters-subgingival-microbiota-and-mmp-8-expression-in-dental-implan-42090680): In brief: In this matched-pair cohort, clinically healthy implants showed MMP-8 levels approximately 29% higher than adjacent homologous teeth (p<0.05). — Brazilian dental journal (DOI: 10.1590/0103-644020266921)
- [Sinus lift in severe atrophy: a composite allograft + PRF protocol delivers histologically mature bone at six months](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/histological-and-histomorphometric-assessment-of-lateral-window-sinus-augmentati-42050510): In brief: In this 60-biopsy single-arm cohort (residual height ≤4 mm), newly formed bone averaged 31.28% at six months — histologically mature with… — BMC oral health (DOI: 10.1186/s12903-026-08332-4)
- [Ridge split vs. bone expansion: which technique costs more crestal bone at 12 months?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/evaluation-and-comparison-of-crestal-bone-loss-around-dental-implants-placed-usi-42064527): In brief: In this 72-site prospective study, conventional drilling produced the least crestal bone loss; ridge split the most, with bone expansion… — Cureus (DOI: 10.7759/cureus.106119)
- [One nanocarrier, three targets: can DNA frameworks redefine adjunctive periodontitis therapy?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/dna-framework-based-nanomedicine-platform-a-triple-function-strategy-for-treatin-42056079): In brief: A tetrahedral DNA nanostructure (tFNA) co-delivers curcumin and defensin, showing antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and osteogenic activity in a… — International journal of oral science (DOI: 10.1038/s41368-026-00439-2)
- [Early peri-implantitis: mechanical debridement alone holds up — adjuncts show no significant edge](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/efficacy-of-different-nonsurgical-treatments-for-peri-implantitis-a-multi-arm-ra-42069893): In brief: In this 6-month RCT (80 implants, 26 patients), all five nonsurgical protocols significantly improved probing depth, bleeding on probing, and… — Scientific reports (DOI: 10.1038/s41598-026-50332-5)
- [Local statins in periodontal therapy: solid evidence for Step 2, limited for surgery](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/adjunctive-use-of-locally-delivered-statins-in-periodontal-therapy-and-pre-impla-42046838): In brief: In Step 2 (non-surgical) therapy, locally delivered statins added ~1.4–2.3 mm PPD reduction over SRP alone across 13 RCTs. — Clinical and experimental dental research (DOI: 10.1002/cre2.70364)
- [Extend GBR fully or not at all: partial coverage fails to protect adjacent alveolar bone](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/effects-of-extended-guided-bone-regeneration-on-the-labial-alveolar-wall-of-adja-42057175): In brief: In this cohort, fully extended GBR (graft to the alveolar crest) prevented horizontal resorption of adjacent teeth's labial wall at 6 months. — Head & face medicine (DOI: 10.1186/s13005-026-00624-y)
- [MRONJ and implants: timing of stem cell therapy determines whether it works at all](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/postoperative-administration-of-mesenchymal-stem-cells-enhances-peri-implant-tis-42070743): In brief: In this rat MRONJ model, preoperative MSC administration produced no measurable benefit over antiresorptive drug treatment alone. — Acta biomaterialia (DOI: 10.1016/j.actbio.2026.04.063)
- [CKD + stage III periodontitis: an RCT to test whether NSPT preserves teeth and masticatory function](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/efficacy-of-periodontal-therapy-in-preventing-tooth-loss-and-improving-masticato-41985956): In brief: This is a protocol paper — no results yet; the RCT is designed to test whether active NSPT + SPC reduces tooth loss over 24 months in CKD… — BMJ open (DOI: 10.1136/bmjopen-2025-103880)
- [After Extraction, Why Do Resorbable Membranes Collapse — and Can a Cortical Lamina Fix That?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/guided-alveolar-ridge-preservation-g-arp-using-a-cortical-lamina-a-pilot-randomi-42041646): In brief: In this pilot RCT, G-ARP with a cortical lamina produced significantly less horizontal ridge resorption than conventional socket preservation. — Dentistry journal (DOI: 10.3390/dj14040193)
- [Probiotics + ozonated oil in periodontitis maintenance: can we reduce microbiological relapse?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/probiotics-and-ozonated-olive-oil-to-maintain-oral-eubiosis-in-stage-i-and-ii-pe-42041656): In brief: In this triple-blind RCT, adjunctive probiotics combined with ozonated olive oil showed more stable subgingival microbiota vs placebo during… — Dentistry journal (DOI: 10.3390/dj14040203)
- [Vitamin D as NSPT adjunct: cautious signal, not a protocol yet](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/the-effectiveness-of-vitamin-d-supplementation-in-association-with-non-surgical-42041664): In brief: RCTs reviewed suggest vitamin D supplementation alongside SRP may improve PPD, CAL, and BOP beyond mechanical therapy alone. — Dentistry journal (DOI: 10.3390/dj14040211)
- [Advanced peri-implantitis in the esthetic zone: a case for staged explantation over early re-implantation](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/management-of-advanced-peri-implantitis-with-staged-explantation-and-delayed-re-42041665): In brief: Staged explantation — remove, decontaminate, GBR, wait, then re-implant — can restore adequate ridge volume before new fixture placement in this… — Dentistry journal (DOI: 10.3390/dj14040212)
- [Stage III/IV Periodontitis: Surgery Wins Short-Term, Maintenance Wins Long-Term](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/comparative-effect-of-non-surgical-versus-surgical-therapy-for-stage-iiiiv-perio-42035718): In brief: In this cohort of 3,194 Chinese patients, SPT produced significantly greater PD reduction (1.16 vs 0.77 mm) and CAL gain (0.97 vs 0.68 mm) than… — International dental journal (DOI: 10.1016/j.identj.2026.109555)
- [Type 2 diabetes and peri-implantitis: no detectable difference in this cohort](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/prevalence-of-peri-implant-diseases-in-patients-with-type-2-diabetes-mellitus-a-42035463): In brief: In 70 patients (35 T2DM / 35 controls, 227 implants), overall peri-implant disease prevalence was 80% vs 77% — not significantly different… — Clinical and experimental dental research (DOI: 10.1002/cre2.70352)
- [Implantoplasty for peri-implantitis: how far into the defect is worth the recession risk?](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/extent-of-implantoplasty-in-the-combined-surgical-therapy-of-peri-implantitis-a-42026938): In brief: In this 33-patient quasi-randomized trial, combined implantoplasty + GBR arrested peri-implantitis progression at 12 months regardless of… — Clinical implant dentistry and related research (DOI: 10.1111/cid.70144)
- [Hyaluronic acid for peri-implantitis: promising biology, not yet a standard adjunct](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/clinical-efficacy-of-hyaluronic-acid-in-peri-implantitis-treatment-a-systematic-42006458): In brief: Six RCTs and controlled studies (110 patients total) show heterogeneous results: some PPD and BOP improvements, others no significant difference… — Biomaterial investigations in dentistry (DOI: 10.2340/biid.v13.45746)
- [Dual fixation for FGG in the posterior mandible: 17% shrinkage and no complications at 12 months](https://osteoperionews.bonebenders.com/en/articles/a-novel-dual-anchoring-technique-for-keratinized-tissue-augmentation-in-posterio-42030755): In brief: In this retrospective series (49 sites), a dual fixation system combining orthodontic anchor screws and membrane tacks reduced FGG shrinkage to… — International dental journal (DOI: 10.1016/j.identj.2026.109593)
